facts.text: Millstone Township takes its name from the Millstone River, named after a millstone that fell into the water during early settlement.
During the Revolution, Millstone hid a secret Continental Army post called Signal Beacon #21, a hidden lookout for the troops.
The township’s first business, the Rising Sun Tavern, hosted George Washington and later British General Sir Henry Clinton on the stagecoach route between Philadelphia and Freehold.
Originally called Somerset Courthouse, the town was the county seat until the British burned it to the ground in 1779 during the war.
The Millstone River’s headwaters begin right in the township, making it a cradle for several New Jersey waterways.
Millstone was officially formed as a township on February 28, 1844, carved from parts of Freehold, Upper Freehold, and Monroe Townships.
